title: EoE Film Budget
wikitext:
[]: EoE Film Budget             Quote Post by rip3mwk » Sun Oct 02, 2005 10:51 pm Anybody out there know the film budget for End of Eva? I have been wondering this for a long time. I know that Princess Mononoke was $20 mil.

[]: Re: EoE Film Budget             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Sun Oct 02, 2005 10:56 pm rip3mwk wrote: Anybody out there know the film budget for End of Eva? I have been wondering this for a long time. I know that Princess Mononoke was $20 mil.

[]:             Quote Post by rip3mwk » Mon Oct 03, 2005 12:06 am It would be nice to have a bit more money/time spent on making the bloodflow smoother (ex: blood skipping over nerv symbol, Misato's blood changing from maroon to bright red in one frame). How about the film gross?

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Mon Oct 03, 2005 3:35 am rip3mwk wrote: It would be nice to have a bit more money/time spent on making the bloodflow smoother (ex: blood skipping over nerv symbol, Misato's blood changing from maroon to bright red in one frame). How about the film gross?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Oct 03, 2005 1:42 pm Steamboy sure is elaborate. But does it have soul? Anyway, Evangelion doesn't need a huge production budget. Although it would have been interesting to see what differences there would be to the Eva designs/story if Gainax had a few million lurking around.

[]:             Quote Post by rip3mwk » Mon Oct 03, 2005 3:23 pm Thanks CH, this has all been helpful... yeah, Otomo definatelly could have gone in a different direction with Steamboy - the film was not very intricate plot-wise. Ah well. I think that it may be a good thing for movies to have a limited budget.

[]:             Quote Post by rip3mwk » Mon Oct 03, 2005 12:06 am It would be nice to have a bit more money/time spent on making the bloodflow smoother (ex: blood skipping over nerv symbol, Misato's blood changing from maroon to bright red in one frame). How about the film gross?
